parts.text:
# Coding Prompt — Another World Simulator

## PROJECT OVERVIEW

Build a web-based prototype of **Another World Simulator**, a TCG (Trading Card Game) simulator/management game inspired by the real "Another World" store in Altamura (BA), Italy. The game blends **pack opening/gacha**, **card trading marketplace**, and **TCG dueling**, with a social multiplayer layer.

Use the following tech stack:
- **Frontend:** React + Tailwind CSS
- **Backend:** Node.js + Express
- **Database:** PostgreSQL (or SQLite for local prototype)
- **Realtime:** Socket.io (for trading, chat, live tournaments)
- **Auth:** JWT-based authentication

---

## VISUAL STYLE

- **Pixel art aesthetic** with anime-inspired elements
- **Color palette:** dark navy backgrounds (`#0a0e1a`), red/gold accents (`#e63946`, `#ffd700`), neon highlights for UI elements
- **Fonts:** pixel-style for headers (e.g. "Press Start 2P"), clean sans-serif for body text
- **Animations:** smooth card flip animations on pack opening, glow effects on rare cards, slide transitions between screens

---

## CORE SYSTEMS TO IMPLEMENT

### 1. Authentication & Player Profile
- Register/Login with username + password
- Player profile: avatar selection, username, rank display, stats (wins/losses, cards owned, AC balance)
- Daily login streak tracker with bonus Another Coins (AC)

### 2. Another Coins (In-Game Currency)
- Every player starts with **500 AC**
- AC is earned through: daily login (5–100 AC based on streak), completing missions, selling cards, winning tournaments
- AC is spent on: packs, avatar skins, deck slots, store accessories
- Display current AC balance persistently in the top navigation bar

### 3. Card System & Collection
- Card data model:
  ```
  Card {
    id, name, tcg (magic|pokemon|onepiece|dragonball),
    rarity (common|uncommon|rare|ultra_rare|legendary),
    image_url, attack, defense, description, market_value
  }
  ```
- Seed the database with at least **20 cards per TCG** (80 total minimum), with varied rarities
- Player collection screen: filterable grid (by TCG, rarity, name), shows quantity owned
- Duplicate cards automatically convert to **card fragments** (10 fragments = 1 random rare)

### 4. Pack Opening (Gacha System)
Three pack types:
| Pack | Cost | Rarity Rates |
|------|------|-------------|
| Standard | 100 AC | 70% common, 25% uncommon, 5% rare |
| Premium | 300 AC | 50% uncommon, 35% rare, 14% ultra_rare, 1% legendary |
| Legendary | 1000 AC | 20% rare, 50% ultra_rare, 30% legendary |

- Each pack reveals **5 cards** with a staged animation:
  - Cards start face-down, flip one by one with a delay
  - Rarity-based glow: silver (uncommon), gold (rare), rainbow (legendary)
  - Sound cue per rarity (use Web Audio API or simple CSS + emoji feedback if no audio)
- **Pity system:** After 10 packs without a rare, next pack guarantees one. Track per player per pack type.
- After opening: option to add cards to collection or quick-sell for AC

### 5. Trading Marketplace
Three trade modes:

**A) Direct 1v1 Trade (realtime via Socket.io)**
- Propose a trade: select cards to offer + cards to request from another online player
- Target player receives a notification and can accept/decline
- On accept: atomic swap of cards between players

**B) Public Auctions**
- List a card for auction with starting price and duration (1h / 6h / 24h)
- Other players bid with AC; highest bid wins when timer expires
- Outbid notification sent to previous bidder
- Active auctions feed visible in Marketplace screen

**C) NPC Daily Deals**
- Every 24h, 3 random NPC offers appear (e.g. "Trade 2 commons for 1 rare")
- Time-limited, refreshes at midnight UTC

Display a **market value** per card based on recent auction sale prices (rolling 7-day average).

### 6. Deck Builder
- Players can create up to 3 decks (extra slots cost 1500 AC)
- Each deck: 20–40 cards, must be all from the same TCG
- Drag-and-drop interface to add/remove cards
- Deck validation: warn if deck is below minimum size
- Save/load/rename/delete decks

### 7. TCG Dueling Engine (simplified)
Implement a **turn-based battle system** for one TCG (start with Pokémon, others can be stubs):

**Pokémon TCG simplified rules:**
- Each player draws 7 cards, places 1 active Pokémon, up to 5 on bench
- Turn structure: draw → play energy/trainer → attack
- Win condition: knock out 3 opponent Pokémon (prize cards)
- Cards have: HP, attack name, attack damage, energy cost
- Keep it functional over complex — no need for full official rule coverage

**Other 3 TCGs (Magic, One Piece, Dragon Ball):** Show a placeholder duel screen with "Coming Soon" and basic card display.

### 8. Tournament System
- **Weekly Casual:** auto-scheduled every Monday/Thursday, open bracket, 4–8 players
- Tournament lobby: join button, waiting room showing registered players
- Single elimination bracket UI
- Winner receives AC prize (defined per tournament type)
- Match results recorded in player history

Tournament types and prizes:
| Type | Prize |
|------|-------|
| Weekly Casual | 100 AC |
| Monthly Championship | 500 AC + exclusive card |
| Ranked Season | Seasonal title + 1000 AC |

### 9. Ranking System
- Per-TCG rank ladder: Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum → Diamond → Champion
- Earn rank points (RP) from tournament wins and ranked duels
- Each rank tier unlocks: avatar frames, card back cosmetics, store areas
- Global leaderboard: top 50 players ranked by combined RP across all TCGs

### 10. Missions & Achievements
**Daily missions (reset at midnight):**
- "Open 2 packs today" → 40 AC
- "Win 1 duel" → 60 AC
- "List a card on the market" → 20 AC

**Weekly missions:**
- "Win 3 tournaments" → 300 AC
- "Complete your Pokémon collection of commons" → 200 AC

**Achievements (100+):**
- First pack opened, First win, Collector (own 50 cards), Legend (reach Champion rank), etc.
- Each grants AC reward on first completion

### 11. Social Features
- **Friend system:** search by username, send/accept friend requests
- **Global chat:** real-time in-store chat via Socket.io, displayed in the Hub Store screen
- **Top Players Board:** leaderboard widget visible on Hub Store home screen
- **Challenge a friend:** directly invite a friend to a duel from their profile

---

## UI SCREENS

Build these screens as separate React route pages:

| Route | Screen | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `/` | Hub Store | Main lobby. Shows store map, global chat, top players board, daily missions widget |
| `/collection` | Collection | Card grid, filters, fragment count |
| `/packs` | Pack Shop | Buy and open packs |
| `/decks` | Deck Builder | Create/manage decks |
| `/market` | Marketplace | Auctions, direct trades, NPC deals |
| `/arena` | Arena | Tournament lobby, duel matchmaking, history |
| `/rankings` | Rankings | Per-TCG leaderboards |
| `/profile` | Profile | Stats, achievements, settings |
| `/login` | Auth | Login / Register |

---

## DATABASE SCHEMA (key tables)

```sql
players (id, username, password_hash, ac_balance, created_at)
cards (id, name, tcg, rarity, image_url, atk, def, description)
player_cards (id, player_id, card_id, quantity)
decks (id, player_id, name, tcg, card_list jsonb)
packs_opened (id, player_id, pack_type, cards_received jsonb, opened_at)
pity_tracker (player_id, pack_type, count_since_last_rare)
auctions (id, seller_id, card_id, start_price, current_bid, bidder_id, ends_at)
trades (id, proposer_id, receiver_id, offered_cards jsonb, requested_cards jsonb, status)
tournaments (id, name, type, scheduled_at, prize_ac, status)
tournament_players (tournament_id, player_id, placement)
friendships (player_id_a, player_id_b, status)
missions (id, type, description, ac_reward, condition jsonb)
player_missions (player_id, mission_id, completed_at)
achievements (id, name, description, ac_reward)
player_achievements (player_id, achievement_id, unlocked_at)
chat_messages (id, player_id, message, sent_at)
```

---

## STARTING STATE & SEED DATA

On first launch / fresh database:
- Seed 80 cards (20 per TCG) with placeholder images (use colored card templates if no art available)
- Create 3 NPC accounts (Il Gestore, Luca il Trader, La Leggenda) with special flags
- Seed 5 daily missions and 5 weekly missions
- Seed 20 starter achievements

New player onboarding flow:
1. Register → choose starter avatar
2. Receive welcome pack: 3 Standard Packs + 200 AC
3. Brief tooltip tutorial (not blocking): show Hub Store → Packs → Collection → Deck Builder

---

## IMPLEMENTATION PRIORITIES

Build in this order:

1. **Auth + player profile + AC system** (foundation)
2. **Card seeding + collection screen** (core content)
3. **Pack opening with gacha + pity** (most engaging feature)
4. **Deck builder** (needed for dueling)
5. **Simplified Pokémon duel engine** (core gameplay)
6. **Marketplace: auctions + direct trades** (social economy)
7. **Tournaments + ranking** (competitive layer)
8. **Missions + achievements** (retention loop)
9. **Social: friends, chat, challenges** (multiplayer polish)
10. **NPC daily deals + narrative flavor** (world-building)

---

## NOTES & CONSTRAINTS

- The game must be **genuinely Free-to-Play**: all cards obtainable without spending real money. Real-money purchases (if implemented) are cosmetics only — never required for competitive play.
- Card rarities must follow the gacha probabilities strictly. Do not allow exploits that let players generate infinite AC.
- The marketplace economy should be player-driven: avoid NPC buyback prices that are too high or it will inflate AC.
- Tournament scheduling can use a simple cron job or a manual "start tournament" admin endpoint for the prototype.
- For card images in the prototype, generate colored placeholder cards with card name and rarity displayed as text — no need for actual card artwork to build the system.
- All realtime features (chat, trade notifications, live auctions) must use Socket.io rooms, not polling.

---

*Based on the Another World Simulator GDD v1.0 — Another World Store, Via dei Mille 88/D, Altamura (BA)*
